s_sub: Read-only update advisor — it never updates anything, it shows what changed and how risky, right in the Docker tab.
s_datadir_hint: SQLite cache — keep on appdata, not the flash
s_ignore_unmanaged_desc: Skip containers not created from an Unraid template (Docker Compose, Dockhand, plain docker run). Only Unraid-managed containers stay in ShipLog.
s_updates_sub: What happens when you click "Update now" in a changelog bubble. ShipLog stays read-only — it triggers Unraid's own update either way.
s_confirm_desc: Show a confirmation prompt before the update starts. Off = one click updates straight away.
s_silent_desc: Run the update without Unraid's pop-up window and its download log. The update still runs in the background.
s_registry_sub: Optional — both raise rate limits so ShipLog can read versions & changelogs for more of your containers. Leave empty to stay anonymous.
s_gh_hint: Reads release notes from GitHub. Anonymous GitHub allows only ~60 requests/hour — with many containers that runs out fast and changelogs come back empty. A classic Personal Access Token (no scopes needed) raises it to ~5000/hour.
s_dh_hint: Checks the newest version + digest of docker.io images. Anonymous Docker Hub is rate-limited per IP — with many images that throttles, so those show an update without a version number. A free Docker Hub access token (read-only) lifts the limit. Username + token, both required.
s_ai_sub: Optional — Ollama condenses each changelog into "what changes / breaking / risk".
s_ollama_url_hint: Probes common local Ollama ports and fills this in.
s_notif_sub: Optional — Matrix message when a new update is first seen.
s_unreachable_hint: — enable it above + Apply, then reload
d_updateHint: triggers Unraid's own update for this container
d_updateGone: Couldn't find Unraid's update button — use 'apply update' on the row.
d_confirmAll: Update all %n containers with a pending update now?
d_updateAllHint: triggers Unraid's own update for every container with a pending update
d_none: No changelog text found for this image — open the repo (top-right) for the release notes.
d_rateLimited: GitHub's hourly rate limit was reached, so the release notes couldn't load. Add a GitHub token in ShipLog's settings (Sources) to raise it.
s_autoupd_sub: scheduled, by version level — Unraid only
s_ca_warn: CA Auto Update is also installed. Both will update containers — use only one to avoid double updates.
s_au_enable_desc: Apply container updates automatically on the schedule below, up to the level you choose. Off by default.
s_au_level_desc: Auto-apply only updates at or below this level. Patch = bug fixes; Minor = new features; Major = may break. Unknown (non-versioned) tags are never auto-updated.
s_au_exclude_desc: Comma-separated words. Blocks an otherwise-eligible update whose changelog text contains any of them (case-insensitive) — e.g. "breaking" catches a release that calls out a breaking change even at a minor or patch bump. Blocked updates are reported, never applied. Empty = off.
s_au_digest_desc: Containers on a rolling tag like :latest carry no version level. Turn this on to auto-apply their digest updates too.
s_au_sched_desc: When to run. Daily runs once at the time you set; At boot runs once after the array is up; Every N hours/days runs on that interval.
s_au_dryrun_desc: Don't apply anything — just send a notification of what WOULD be updated. Use this first to see the plan before arming real updates.
